nodes.label: E. Howard Hunt (extended)
nodes.summary: CIA officer, Bay of Pigs action officer; one of the Watergate burglars; author of at least 3 occult novels (one thinly-veiled attack on the Kennedys as Satan worshippers); lived on 'Witches Island'.

nodes.label: Levenda discovered E. Howard Hunt wrote at least 3 occult novels — one thinly-veiled attack framing
nodes.summary: Levenda discovered E. Howard Hunt wrote at least 3 occult novels — one thinly-veiled attack framing the Kennedys as Satan worshippers, reflecting Hunt's Bay-of-Pigs-era vitriol.

sources.quote: E. Howard Hunt wrote at least three occult novels. It's an attack on the Kennedys, the Kennedys as Satan worshippers, thinly veiled kind of a story about that. The vitriol that he had for the Kennedys over Bay of Pigs was palpable.

nodes.label: Levenda recounts: on his first Watergate/Nazi-occultism research trip to the National Archives (1970

Episode: CIA Historian: “Aliens Are Controlling Human Timelines!” (ft. Peter Levenda)
Levenda discovered E. Howard Hunt wrote at least 3 occult novels — one thinly-veiled attack framing the Kennedys as Satan worshippers, reflecting Hunt's Bay-of-Pigs-era vitriol.
E. Howard Hunt lived on a place literally named 'Witches Island' — an on-the-nose detail that reinforces Levenda's thesis that mundane American political history is suffused with occult structure.
Levenda posits: 'If we have a crash saucer from the 40s, and they have one too, there is an agreement between countries that says we're not going to talk about this — it's Fight Club.'
Per Levenda: E. Howard Hunt on his deathbed admitted knowing about a conspiracy to kill Kennedy — placing Hunt among the last CIA voices confirming JFK was assassinated over 'the alien presence.'
